name: Hub keeps enforcing a DERControl past its expiry
hypothesis: The grid server issues a short export-cap DERControl that expires mid-window. Past validUntil (+grace) the control is dead and the hub must release it. A hub that keeps enforcing an expired control needlessly curtails the inverter forever — lost generation the utility never asked for.
expected: Drop the control at validUntil + grace: stop adopting it and let the inverter recover. Never enforce a DERControl past its expiry.
fix: Expire a DERControl at validUntil (+grace) and release its setpoint (csip scheduler Evaluate / orchestrator).
notes: TASK-077: migrated from the Go literal in cmd/dashboard/mayhem.go scenarios() (ID expired-control). Short-lived export cap: validUntil ~= +30 s, so it expires ~30 s into the 90 s window and the +grace boundary (~60 s) leaves a clear tail to catch a hub that keeps enforcing it. Uses post_control directly (not the constraint sugar) so the author-declared typ/lim_w land on the resulting activeConstraint exactly as the Go twin's `&activeConstraint{Typ: "exportCap", LimW: 0, MRID: mrid}` does.

body.description: mayhem: short-lived export cap that must expire
